title: Central-Madrid monthly meteorology (temperature, wind speed + direction components, precipitation) for the Madrid Central controlled-ITS, 2017-01 to 2019-12
publisher: Open-Meteo (ERA5 reanalysis, Copernicus Climate Change Service)
licence: CC BY 4.0 (Open-Meteo); contains modified Copernicus Climate Change Service information (ERA5)
vintage: Open-Meteo historical-weather (ERA5) archive for a central-Madrid point (40.4168, -3.7038), daily values aggregated to monthly means: temperature_2m_mean (C), wind_speed_10m_mean (km/h), direction-resolved wind components u/v (km/h), precipitation_sum (mm). One row per month over 2017-01..2019-12.
notes: Meteorology is the dominant short-window NO2 confounder. In the controlled ITS the municipal urban/suburban-background control series already absorbs most shared dispersion, so this join enters as the meteorology-adjusted specification arm of the model average. Wind is stored as u/v components so a monthly mean respects direction.
